workflows.scope: Roadmap-driven product work and milestone continuation.
validation_commands: wsl.exe bash -lc 'cd /home/fa507/dev/accessible_reader/backend && .venv/bin/python -c "from app.main import app; print(app.title)"'

workflows.scope: Resume the active roadmap and current checkpoint without guessing.
validation_commands:
wsl.exe bash -lc 'cd /home/fa507/dev/accessible_reader && python3 - <<"PY"
import json
from pathlib import Path
json.loads(Path("docs/assistant/manifest.json").read_text())
print("manifest ok")
PY'

workflows.scope: Edge read-aloud, sentence highlighting, progress restore, and manual browser validation.
Apply the vendored profile-driven harness to repo-local assistant docs.

contracts.template_read_policy: Read docs/assistant/templates/* only when the user explicitly asks for harness/bootstrap prompt creation, explicit project harness sync or audit work, cross-project Codex scaffolding, or a follow-up delta/refinement prompt.
contracts.delta_refinement_prompt_policy: When the user explicitly asks for a follow-up delta/refinement prompt based on later prototype or live-review learnings, use docs/assistant/templates/CODEX_DELTA_REFINEMENT_PROMPT.md and keep the pass inspect-first: keep good work, correct weak work, add only the meaningful delta.
contracts.session_resume_entry: Use docs/assistant/workflows/SESSION_RESUME.md when the user asks where to resume or wants the next roadmap restart point.
contracts.project_harness_sync_policy: Use docs/assistant/workflows/PROJECT_HARNESS_SYNC_WORKFLOW.md when the user explicitly asks to implement, sync, audit, or check the project harness.
contracts.harness_profile_policy: Treat docs/assistant/HARNESS_PROFILE.json and docs/assistant/runtime/BOOTSTRAP_STATE.json as repo-local harness inputs and preview outputs.
contracts.wsl_toolchain_home: Run git, npm, pytest, and uvicorn from WSL against /home/fa507/dev/accessible_reader.
contracts.browser_target: Validate browser behavior in Windows Edge on Windows 11.
contracts.browser_bridge_scope: The browser app is the primary surface and the Edge extension is a supported context-only companion surface backed by the localhost Recall API.
contracts.local_first_default: Keep parsing, storage, search, settings, progress, and deterministic reflow local-first.
contracts.web_import_scope: Public webpage import is limited to article-style pages, fetched once, stored locally as snapshots, and never live-synced on reopen.
contracts.speech_path: Browser-native speechSynthesis is the shipped read-aloud path for v1.
contracts.local_tts_status: Local TTS remains deferred and should be described as coming soon unless the user reprioritizes it.
contracts.ai_scope: AI is opt-in only and limited to Simplify and Summary.
contracts.reader_generated_content_lock: In the current Recall-parity track, Reader is original-only and cosmetic-only. Do not change Reflowed, Simplified, Summary, generated-view UX, transform logic, generated placeholders, generated-view controls, or mode-routing unless the user explicitly reprioritizes generated-content work.
contracts.ux_priority: Prefer the best Recall-quality workflow over preserving the current UI structure; preserve local-first behavior, routes, anchors, and reading continuity instead of freezing layout.
contracts.validation_strategy: Run targeted validation before broad checks; keep backend pytest targeted when backend code is untouched; for Recall shell or top-level UI work, require fresh screenshot artifacts, use targeted Vitest coverage first, and keep the broad App.test.tsx pass plus repo-owned Edge harnesses available for shell, route, and visual continuity checks. If App.test.tsx ever appears to stall again, inspect App-level callback identity churn and ReaderWorkspace effect dependencies before treating the file as an unreliable runner.
contracts.push_policy: Do not push unless the user explicitly asks.
contracts.execplan_policy: Major or multi-file work must start by creating or updating an ExecPlan in docs/exec_plans/active/.
contracts.desktop_first_queue_policy: Keep one active desktop-first milestone at a time. The Stage 380 audit reset the current Recall-parity track to Graph, Home, and original-only Reader, and the repo has since carried that track through the completed local Stage 498-564 continuity ladder. Stage 564 is the latest completed audit, Stage 563 is the latest implementation checkpoint, and the queue is back in refreshed-baseline hold again: keep Graph, Home, and original-only Reader stable unless the user explicitly reopens product work or a direct regression forces a detour. If another bounded slice opens, Home remains the likeliest next target. Notes and Study remain parked regression baselines, generated-content Reader work stays locked, and docs/exec_plans/active/ may still contain older or pre-staged plans, so use docs/ROADMAP_ANCHOR.md, agent.md, and docs/assistant/INDEX.md to identify the current checkpoint.
